req.title: KYC refresh (negative variant)
req.statement: A customer shall complete a KYC refresh every 365 days; accounts beyond refresh window shall be restricted to inbound transactions only. Negative paths (insufficient funds, unauthorised, exceeded limits) must be tested.

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-065 mandates KYC refresh every 365 days with account restrictions beyond that window. The suite covers: (1) EP positive — a compliant authenticated customer submits valid KYC data within the 365-day window and receives verified=true; (2) NEG negative — a customer whose account is already beyond the 365-day window attempts an outbound transaction and receives a 403 restriction; (3) BVA boundary — KYC refresh submitted exactly on day 365 (the edge of the allowed window) to confirm acceptance at the boundary; (4) DT negative — submission fails compliance check (e.g. missing required document) and the API returns 403 per the error pathway; (5) STC state-transition — customer transitions from unverified/expired state to verified state after a successful refresh, confirming customer.verified=true is set post-submission. Each test is self-contained via fixtures that seed and tear down account state.

tests.name: test_kyc_refresh_valid_submission_returns_verified
preconditions: Customer account exists and is authenticated
Last KYC refresh was fewer than 365 days ago
Valid KYC payload with all required documents is prepared
actions: Authenticate as customer and obtain bearer token
POST /kyc/refresh with a complete, valid KYC payload
expected: Response body contains a refreshed timestamp within today's date
trace: R-FIN-065: successful KYC refresh sets customer.verified=true
R-FIN-065: positive path — compliant submission accepted
tests.executable:
def test_kyc_refresh_valid_submission_returns_verified(auth_token, valid_kyc_payload, api_client):
    resp = api_client.post(
        "/kyc/refresh",
        json=valid_kyc_payload,
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {auth_token}"},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["customer"]["verified"] is True
    assert "refreshed" in body["customer"]

tests.name: test_kyc_refresh_expired_account_outbound_transaction_returns_403
preconditions: Customer account exists and is authenticated
Last KYC refresh timestamp is more than 365 days in the past (account in restricted state)
Customer attempts an outbound transaction payload
actions: Authenticate as customer with expired KYC and obtain bearer token
POST /kyc/refresh with an outbound transaction request on the restricted account
expected: Response body contains an error code indicating account restriction
trace: R-FIN-065: accounts beyond refresh window restricted to inbound transactions only
R-FIN-065: error pathway — 403 when submission fails compliance
tests.executable:
def test_kyc_refresh_expired_account_outbound_transaction_returns_403(
    expired_kyc_auth_token, outbound_transaction_payload, api_client
):
    resp = api_client.post(
        "/kyc/refresh",
        json=outbound_transaction_payload,
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {expired_kyc_auth_token}"},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 403
    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body
    assert body["error"]["code"] == "ACCOUNT_RESTRICTED"

tests.name: test_kyc_refresh_on_exact_day_365_is_accepted
preconditions: Customer account exists and is authenticated
Last KYC refresh timestamp is set to exactly 365 days ago (boundary value)
actions: Seed account so that last_kyc_refresh = today - 365 days
POST /kyc/refresh with a valid KYC payload
expected: HTTP 200 response (day 365 is the last valid day, inclusive)
trace: R-FIN-065: customer shall complete KYC refresh every 365 days
BVA: exact boundary at 365-day threshold must be accepted
tests.executable:
import datetime

def test_kyc_refresh_on_exact_day_365_is_accepted(
    api_client, valid_kyc_payload, seed_kyc_refresh_date, get_auth_token
):
    boundary_date = datetime.date.today() - datetime.timedelta(days=365)
    customer_id = seed_kyc_refresh_date(boundary_date)
    token = get_auth_token(customer_id)
    resp = api_client.post(
        "/kyc/refresh",
        json=valid_kyc_payload,
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["customer"]["verified"] is True

tests.name: test_kyc_refresh_missing_required_document_returns_403
preconditions: Customer account exists and is authenticated
KYC payload is missing a mandatory compliance document (e.g. proof_of_address)
Account is within the 365-day refresh window
actions: Authenticate as customer and obtain bearer token
POST /kyc/refresh with an incomplete KYC payload (missing proof_of_address)
expected: Response body contains error code COMPLIANCE_FAILURE
trace: DT: decision — missing mandatory document => compliance failure branch
tests.executable:
def test_kyc_refresh_missing_required_document_returns_403(
    auth_token, api_client, valid_kyc_payload
):
    incomplete_payload = {k: v for k, v in valid_kyc_payload.items()
                          if k != "proof_of_address"}
    resp = api_client.post(
        "/kyc/refresh",
        json=incomplete_payload,
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {auth_token}"},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 403
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["error"]["code"] == "COMPLIANCE_FAILURE"
    assert "proof_of_address" in body["error"].get("details", "")

tests.name: test_kyc_refresh_transitions_account_from_restricted_to_active
preconditions: Customer account is in RESTRICTED state (KYC expired > 365 days ago)
Customer submits a fully valid KYC refresh payload
actions: Seed account in RESTRICTED state with expired KYC
POST /kyc/refresh with a complete, valid KYC payload
Assert account is now in ACTIVE state
expected: Outbound transactions are no longer blocked
trace: R-FIN-065: customer.verified=true refreshed after successful submission
STC: state transition RESTRICTED -> ACTIVE upon successful KYC refresh
tests.executable:
def test_kyc_refresh_transitions_account_from_restricted_to_active(
    api_client, valid_kyc_payload, seed_expired_kyc_account, get_auth_token
):
    customer_id = seed_expired_kyc_account(days_expired=400)
    token = get_auth_token(customer_id)
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"}
    refresh_resp = api_client.post(
        "/kyc/refresh", json=valid_kyc_payload, headers=headers
    )
    assert refresh_resp.status_code == 200
    assert refresh_resp.json()["customer"]["verified"] is True
    status_resp = api_client.get("/account/status", headers=headers)
    assert status_resp.status_code == 200
    assert status_resp.json()["account"]["state"] == "ACTIVE"
